name: Ruby Braff
abstract: Reuben "Ruby" Braff (March 16, 1927 – February 9, 2003) was an American jazz trumpeter and cornetist. Jack Teagarden was once asked about him on the Garry Moore television show and described Ruby as "the Ivy League Louis Armstrong".

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript contains only biographical notes about Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins. Ruby Braff is mentioned only as a colleague of Tommy Newsom ('worked in a swing sextet with Ruby Braff'). The interviewee does not mention Ruby Braff, nor is there any text indicating a personal relationship betwee
about_quotes.doc_title: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Tate
about_quotes.evidence: I was in his trio for three, a couple, two, three years. ... We made some nice records and did some good gigs and concerts.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Tate interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: we had a little group called the Newport Jazz Festival All Stars, which was Ruby Braff, who has become a brilliant player, Pee Wee Russell, clarinet, George played the piano

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: we added Ruby Braff on cornet who was one of my co-musicians... Ruby was an important influence in my life and I guess I was the most important influence on his life
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Moore
about_quotes.evidence: one of the bands that I worked with in my young years which was so important to me which was George Barnes and Ruby Braff, the quartet record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Moore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Polcer
about_quotes.evidence: The intensity of certain players, Muggsy Spanier and the beautiful intensity of Ruby Braff. Those are probably the strongest influences on me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Polcer interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998
